---
title: "v3 Phase D: data additions"
subtitle: "Magnetic + gravity wiring (already upstream), NHD VAA, CGS Jennings scaffold, OSM mining, motherlode A/B"
author: "Sky King"
date: 2026-06-02
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
execute:
  warning: false
---

## What landed in Phase D

Five workflow agents in parallel grew the per-population feature stacks from
(17, 18) to (18, 23) without expensive new data fetches. Most additions are
cheap (wiring rasters already on disk; reading attributes already in the
GeoPackages we own).

```{python}
#| label: feature-growth
from ai_minerals.config import (
    TERTIARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS, QUATERNARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS,
)
print(f"Tertiary feature stack:   {len(TERTIARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS)} features")
print(f"Quaternary feature stack: {len(QUATERNARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS)} features")
print()
print("Features added in Phase D:")
phase_d_features = {
    "motherlode_prob (D.5)": ("motherlode_prob" in TERTIARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS,
                              "motherlode_prob" in QUATERNARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS),
    "nearest_reach_stream_order (D.2)": (False, "nearest_reach_stream_order" in QUATERNARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS),
    "nearest_reach_arbolate_sum_km (D.2)": (False, "nearest_reach_arbolate_sum_km" in QUATERNARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS),
    "nearest_reach_slope (D.2)": (False, "nearest_reach_slope" in QUATERNARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS),
    "distance_to_nearest_reach_m (D.2)": (False, "distance_to_nearest_reach_m" in QUATERNARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS),
}
for feat, (in_t, in_q) in phase_d_features.items():
    print(f"  {feat:55s} tert={in_t}, quat={in_q}")
```

## D.1 magnetic + gravity wiring

The Region's `raw_paths` dict already included `magnetic` and `gravity`
(mirrored from the motherlode pipeline; same AOI). `build_feature_frame` in
`src/ai_minerals/features/assemble.py` already samples both rasters onto the
grid and attaches as `df["magnetic"]` and `df["gravity"]` BEFORE the placer-
specific column build runs. So D.1's "wire magnetic + gravity into placer"
turned out to be a verify-only task — they were already there.

The assemble script gets a documenting "Geophysics" section as a defensive
check so a regression upstream surfaces as a warning rather than silently
dropping the features.

## D.2 NHDPlus HR ValueAddedAttributes

Four cell-level features sampled from the nearest NHD reach:

- `nearest_reach_stream_order`: Strahler stream order (1 = headwater, 7 = main
  stem). Quaternary placers concentrate on orders 3-5 (gold settles where
  stream power transitions from "scour" to "deposit").
- `nearest_reach_arbolate_sum_km`: upstream drainage length, in km. Catchment
  size proxy.
- `nearest_reach_slope`: longitudinal slope (dimensionless). Knickpoint /
  flat-stretch indicator. Currently NaN-dominated until the fetcher is re-run
  with the slope column extension (added in this commit).
- `distance_to_nearest_reach_m`: cell-to-reach Euclidean distance.

All four added to QUATERNARY_FEATURE_COLUMNS only. Tertiary cells on hillside
benches above modern drainage are far from any NHD reach by definition; the
features would be NaN-dominated and add noise.

## D.3 CGS Jennings 2010 fault scaffold (wiring deferred to v3.5)

The v3 plan called for splitting v2's single `distance_to_fault_m` feature
into two: pre-Quaternary faults (lode control for Tertiary) and Quaternary-
active faults (modern drainage tilt for Quaternary). v3 ships the scaffold
without the assemble integration:

```{python}
#| label: cgs-jennings-scaffold
from ai_minerals.data.cgs_jennings import fetch_cgs_jennings
from ai_minerals.data.adapters import get_adapter
from ai_minerals.features.hydrology import distance_to_fault_by_class
import inspect

print(f"fetcher:        {fetch_cgs_jennings.__name__}")
print(f"adapter:        {get_adapter('geology', 'cgs_jennings').__name__}")
print(f"feature fn sig: {inspect.signature(distance_to_fault_by_class)}")
```

The fetcher pages the CGS FaultActivityMapCA FeatureServer with bbox clipping
to the region AOI. The adapter normalizes the activity-class column into
`pre_quaternary` and `quaternary_active`. The feature function returns a dict
mapping each class to a per-cell distance Series. v3.5 work: actually call the
fetcher, run the assemble integration, add `distance_to_prequaternary_fault_m`
and `distance_to_quaternary_fault_m` to the per-population feature stacks.

The fetcher was NOT called from the workflow agent (would hit the live CGS
server from an unattended job). Trigger manually:

```bash
.venv/bin/python -c "from ai_minerals.data.cgs_jennings import fetch_cgs_jennings; \
    from ai_minerals.regions.northern_sierra_placer import NORTHERN_SIERRA_PLACER; \
    fetch_cgs_jennings(NORTHERN_SIERRA_PLACER.aoi)"
```

## D.4 OSM mining-tag features as supplementary Quaternary labels

New `scripts/northern_sierra_placer/fetch_osm_mining.py` queries OSM Overpass
via osmnx for `historic=mine`, `man_made=mineshaft`, `man_made=adit`,
`landuse=quarry` tags within the placer AOI. Results written to
`data/raw/osm_mining/osm_mining_<region>.gpkg`.

Used as **supplementary positive labels** for the Quaternary population
(modern-stream placers concentrate on the OSM mining tag set). Gated by the
new `--augment-osm-mining` CLI flag in `assemble_250m.py` (default off so
the v3 retrain in Phase F.1 doesn't accidentally pick up label augmentation
without explicit opt-in).

ODbL attribution requirement documented in the fetcher's header.

## D.5 motherlode_prob A/B diagnostic feature

The motherlode v2 calibrated raster at
`data/derived/motherlode/prospectivity_motherlode_v2_250m_calibrated_4326.tif`
overlaps the placer AOI substantially. v3 samples it onto each placer cell as a
single feature `motherlode_prob`. Added to BOTH Tertiary and Quaternary stacks.

The question: does feeding the lode model's output into the placer model help
generalization or just compound errors? v3 ships an A/B comparison via the
new `--no-motherlode-prob` CLI flag in `train_predict_250m.py` main(). Default
includes motherlode_prob; flag drops it from feat_cols before fitting. Phase
F.1 will train both variants and compare per-fold AUC, anchor gate, Lindgren
held-out.

The proper hierarchical model (out-of-fold motherlode predictions per placer
spatial-block fold so the motherlode model never sees placer-fold test cells)
is v4 work. v3's single-feature approach is a diagnostic.

## What's next (Phase E)

Six validation upgrades, parallelizable:

1. **E.1** per-population calibration validation (binned predicted P vs
   observed positive rate)
2. **E.2** per-fold capture rates (top-k%) alongside AUC
3. **E.3** Platt vs isotonic calibration ablation
4. **E.4** SHAP feature importance + recipe-match check
5. **E.5** leakage-risk audit script (per-feature × per-fold correlation
   between feature and held-out label)
6. **E.6** spatial block size empirical justification (10/15/20/25/30 km
   ablation)
